title: core/pysrc/_gitexec.py trust-boundary validation has no direct unit test
evidence: _gitexec.py's `_trusted_environment_path()` is the trust boundary that decides whether `CODEARBITER_GIT_EXECUTABLE`/`CODEARBITER_PYTHON_EXECUTABLE` env values are honored as the Git/Python executable identity for every shared Git subprocess: it requires an absolute path (`os.path.isabs`), resolves symlinks (`os.path.realpath`), and requires the resolved target to exist (`os.path.isfile`) before trusting it, else raising. Searching the whole `.github/scripts/` suite for `_gitexec`/`trusted_git_executable`/`CODEARBITER_GIT_EXECUTABLE` finds only two references, both structural/grep assertions on other files (`test_pi_package.py:964` asserts no source file calls a raw git subprocess instead of `_gitexec.git_executable()`; `test_migration_backstop.py:280` excludes lines containing the string `_gitexec` from a different scan). No test constructs `_gitexec.py` scenarios (relative path input, missing-file input, symlink resolution, unset env var) and asserts `_trusted_environment_path`/`trusted_git_executable`/`git_executable` behavior directly.
impact: A regression that weakens this validation (e.g. accepting a relative path, skipping the isfile check, or the realpath symlink resolution) would silently change which git/python binary every shared Git subprocess across ca, ca-codex, and ca-pi trusts, with no test catching it.
recommendation: Add a direct unit test for core/pysrc/_gitexec.py covering: absent env var (falls back to bare 'git'/None), relative path (raises), nonexistent absolute path (raises), and a symlink resolving via realpath to a real file (trusted, canonicalized).
acceptance_criteria: A test file directly imports core/pysrc/_gitexec.py and exercises _trusted_environment_path's four branches (absent, relative, missing, valid-symlink)
